channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: The FDA’s flavored vape ban was a shortsighted disaster
item.description: The debate over flavoured e-cigarettes is once again gaining momentum as regulators reconsider how best to address nicotine use in modern society. What began as a public health effort to curb youth vaping has evolved into a more complex discussion about unintended consequences and policy effectiveness. Questions are now being raised about whether strict regulations have truly reduced harm or simply shifted it into less controlled spaces. At the same time, the role of e-cigarettes as a potential alternative for adult smokers remains highly contested. This issue ultimately reflects a broader challenge of balancing public health priorities with individual choice in an evolving nicotine landscape. (Published on March 18, 2026bywashingtonpost.com)

item.title: GFN News #183 | GERMANY'S VAPE FLAVOUR CRACKDOWN?
item.description: In this episode of GFN News, Joanna Junak speaks with Will Godfrey of Filter about Germany’s proposed regulation to ban flavoured e-liquids containing cooling agents. The draft measure would prohibit menthol and other “iced” vape products that rely on cooling additives. Supporters of the proposal, including Germany’s Federal Institute for Risk Assessment, argue that cooling effects may make vaping easier to inhale and potentially increase nicotine intake - particularly among young and inexperienced users. (Published on March 18, 2026bygfn.tv)

item.title: Nearly half of Europe’s vape market comes from irregular sources, study finds
item.description: A study by Fraunhofer Institute reveals that almost half of vapes in Europe stem from irregular sources, amounting to a €6.6 billion market. The research, tracking supply chains and customs data, unveils links to illegal transactions. China dominates as the main supplier, particularly from Shenzhen, with 90% of products coming from there. Germany, the Netherlands, and Belgium serve as key distribution centers. Enforcement faces challenges due to increasing parcel volumes. These irregular trades cause tax losses and pressure legitimate retailers, with bans likely to boost illicit market growth. Recommendations include digital tracking systems and enhanced cooperation to combat the issue. (Published on March 18, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: Farsalinos Reviews Pouch Evidence
item.description: A recent study by Dr. Konstantinos Farsalinos shows that modern tobacco-free nicotine pouches are among the least harmful nicotine products in terms of chemical exposure. The research indicates that harmful chemicals in these pouches are largely undetectable or present at negligible levels compared to cigarettes. Published in Internal and Emergency Medicine, the paper highlights that nicotine pouches occupy the lowest end of the toxicant risk continuum. Harmful constituents, including tobacco-specific nitrosamines, are minimal compared to both snus and combustible cigarettes. As a result, smokers who switch to nicotine pouches can achieve reductions in toxicant exposure similar to those seen with complete smoking cessation. (Published on March 18, 2026byplanetofthevapes.co.uk)

item.title: Complementing and expanding Chris Snowdon's latest Substack on passive vaping
item.description: This is the second post (see first post) complementing and expanding the excellent Substack by Chris Snowdon on the weakness of evidence against risks of “passive vaping” (exposure to “second hand” or the aerosol exhaled by vapers). This Substack post continues my previous critique of studies mentioned by Chris. I will examine what happens under special conditions with multiple vapers producing a large mass of exhaled vaping aerosol (EVA). [...] (Published on March 18, 2026bysubstack.com)
